skill.name: code-review-expert
skill.description: Structured code review guidance for architecture, security, quality, and release readiness checks.
skill.summary: Guides AI assistants through structured code reviews with severity-based findings.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static findings were evaluated against SKILL.md and no confirmed security issue was found. The backtick findings at SKILL.md lines 43 and 49 are Markdown code fence delimiters, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive from descriptive text, with no cryptographic operation present.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Code Fence
low_findings.description: The backtick markers at SKILL.md lines 43 and 49 open and close a Markdown code block. They do not execute Ruby, shell commands, or user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown formatting around review phase text. No executable script, command invocation, or interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 3 contains a natural language description of code review triggers. No hash, cipher, digest, or cryptographic algorithm usage appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter description text and contains no cryptographic API or algorithm name. The static high-risk label is not supported by file context.

content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss architecture, security, and quality issues. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured review phases.
actual_capabilities: Provides a five-phase code review workflow from initial scan to final report.
Guides architecture, module, and function-level review in a top-down sequence.
Prompts review from architect, product, QA, and UX perspectives.
Directs deeper checks for security and performance concerns.
Uses severity levels to prioritize findings before deployment.
Includes a quick checklist for common TypeScript and backend review issues.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, scanners, or automated analysis tools.
Requires the user to provide code, diffs, or repository context for review.
Does not replace tests, linters, static analyzers, or security scanners.
Severity guidance is generic and may need adjustment for team policy.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request Before Merge
use_cases.description: Apply a structured checklist to identify security, quality, and maintainability issues before approval.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Architecture Changes
use_cases.description: Assess module boundaries, system impact, and design tradeoffs before committing to a technical direction.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Readiness Feedback
use_cases.description: Prioritize findings by severity so teams know which issues must be fixed before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code change for correctness, readability, and obvious bugs. Use clear severity levels and include file references when available.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard review before merging a feature branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request using the five review phases. Focus on architecture, security, performance, tests, and maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a design or refactor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this architecture change from architect, product, QA, and UX perspectives. Identify risks, tradeoffs, and missing validation.

prompt_templates.title: Release Gate Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a release readiness review. Prioritize critical and high findings first, then list medium and low items with practical remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that adds a new authentication flow.
output: Critical: Missing validation on token audience before accepting login responses.
High: Error handling leaks internal provider details to clients.
Medium: Tests cover success paths but omit expired token and replay cases.

output_examples.input: Assess a service refactor before merging.
output: Architecture: The new boundary reduces controller complexity but adds unclear ownership for retry behavior.
Quality: The naming is consistent, but the migration path needs rollback documentation.
Action: Add integration tests around timeout and partial failure scenarios.

best_practices: Provide the relevant diff, surrounding files, and product intent before asking for review.
Ask for severity-ranked findings when decisions affect release timing.
Combine this skill with tests, linters, and security tools for higher confidence.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for approval without sharing the code or architecture context.
Do not treat generic severity labels as a substitute for team policy.
Do not skip verification after applying recommended fixes.

faq.question: Does this skill run automated code scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and checklists, but it does not run scanners or scripts.

faq.question: Can it review security issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It prompts deeper review of security concerns, but specialist testing may still be needed.

faq.question: What code should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the diff, affected files, expected behavior, and any known constraints.

faq.question: Can it help with architecture reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides top-down review from architecture to modules and functions.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: How should I use the severity levels?
faq.answer: Use them to prioritize action, then map them to your team release policy.
